01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.stream.consistency.check.enabled
01-client-fs-io.description: When this is enabled, Alluxio guarantees data consistency for the life cycle of a data stream. If the file contents changes during an ongoing stream, the stream will fail. And the caller should retry reading the whole file.

01-client-fs-io.description: When this is enabled, if a data stream fails on observing data inconsistency, the client will best-effort notify all relevant workers about a potential data version change. Consequently, the cluster will drop out-of-date cache and the next read will successfully observe latest file contents.

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.master.client.pool.gc.interval

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.master.client.pool.gc.threshold
01-client-fs-io.description: A fs master client is closed if it has been idle for more than this threshold.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.load.type
01-client-fs-io.description: The behavior of loading metadata from UFS. When information about a path is requested and the path does not exist in Alluxio, metadata can be loaded from the UFS. Valid options are `ALWAYS`, `NEVER`, and `ONCE`. `ALWAYS` will always access UFS to see if the path exists in the UFS. `NEVER` will never consult the UFS. `ONCE` will access the UFS the "first" time (according to a cache), but not after that. This parameter is ignored if a metadata sync is performed, via the parameter "alluxio.user.file.metadata.sync.interval"

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.refresh.ahead.margin.ms
01-client-fs-io.description: The time margin between when to refresh a file's metadata and when the snapshot of the file's metadata expires. The ahead-of-time refresh will reduce the chance when the snapshot has not yet expired, but is very close to expiry, and will probably expire considering the network delay and queuing times, etc. when a request is processed by the worker.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.sync.interval
01-client-fs-io.description: The interval for syncing UFS metadata before invoking an operation on a path. -1 means no sync will occur. 0 means Alluxio will always sync the metadata of the path before an operation. If you specify a time interval, Alluxio will (best effort) not re-sync a path within that time interval. Syncing the metadata for a path must interact with the UFS, so it is an expensive operation. If a sync is performed for an operation, the configuration of "alluxio.user.file.metadata.load.type" will be ignored.

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.real.content.hash

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.passive.cache.enabled

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.readtype.default
01-client-fs-io.description: Default read type when creating Alluxio files. Valid options are `CACHE_PROMOTE` (move data to highest tier if already in Alluxio storage, write data into highest tier of local Alluxio if data needs to be read from under storage), `CACHE` (write data into highest tier of local Alluxio if data needs to be read from under storage), `NO_CACHE` (no data interaction with Alluxio, if the read is from Alluxio data migration or eviction will not occur).

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.persist.on.rename
01-client-fs-io.description: Whether or not to asynchronously persist any files which have been renamed. This is helpful when working with compute frameworks which use rename to commit results.

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.persistence.initial.wait.time
01-client-fs-io.description: Time to wait before starting the persistence job. When the value is set to -1, the file will be persisted by rename operation or persist CLI but will not be automatically persisted in other cases. This is to avoid the heavy object copy in rename operation when %s is set to %s. This value should be smaller than the value of %s

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.segment.enabled
01-client-fs-io.description: Whether to enabled segmented read of files, possibly from multiple workers. If enabled, read requests will be split into segments, and data is pulled from multiple workers. Note this currently only works with read-only workloads.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.segment.size
01-client-fs-io.description: Size of a file segment that will be cached and read independently on multiple workers. Note for a given file, all clients must agree on a common segment size, otherwise they will make incompatible decisions about where to store and retrieve the segments of the file. Segment size should be a multiplier of

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.block.read.metrics.enabled
01-client-fs-io.description: Whether detailed block read metrics will be recorded and sink.

01-client-fs-io.description: When set to true, this property enables optimized gRPC communication between client and workers, leveraging gRPC built-in functions to multiplex connections and handle inactive resources.

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.default.delete.all.replicas
01-client-fs-io.description: If true, the client will attempt to find all replicas of a file and delete them. Otherwise, the client will only delete from the main worker for the path according to consistent hash. This configuration is recommended if Alluxio is running with multiple replicas for each file.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.writetype.default
01-client-fs-io.description: Default write type when creating Alluxio files. Valid options are `CACHE_THROUGH` (try to cache, write to UnderFS synchronously), `THROUGH` (no cache, write to UnderFS synchronously)

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.file.write.tier.default
01-client-fs-io.description: The default tier for choosing a where to write a block. Valid option is any integer. Non-negative values identify tiers starting from top going down (0 identifies the first tier, 1 identifies the second tier, and so on). If the provided value is greater than the number of tiers, it identifies the last tier. Negative values identify tiers starting from the bottom going up (-1 identifies the last tier, -2 identifies the second to last tier, and so on). If the absolute value of the provided value is greater than the number of tiers, it identifies the first tier.

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.streaming.data.read.timeout
01-client-fs-io.description: The maximum time for an Alluxio client to wait for a data response for read requests from Alluxio worker. Keep in mind that some streaming operations may take an unexpectedly long time, such as UFS io. In order to handle occasional slow operations, it is recommended for this parameter to be set to a large value, to avoid spurious timeouts.

01-client-fs-io.description: When a client reads from a remote worker, the maximum number of messages to buffer by the client. A message can be either a command response, a data chunk, or a gRPC stream event such as complete or error.

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.streaming.reader.chunk.size.bytes

01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.worker.selection.policy.type
01-client-fs-io.description: Optional override of applied only when selecting workers for the write cache path. When unset, the write cache reuses the read-side policy. Set to to spread writes toward workers with the most remaining pinned-cache capacity.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.top.fraction
01-client-fs-io.description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. The fraction [0, 1] of workers, ranked by remaining pinned-cache capacity, that always stay in the candidate pool (set to 0 to disable the floor and rely solely on the inclusion threshold). The emptiest workers up to this fraction are kept even when most of the cluster is filling up, so writes concentrate on the least-full nodes under pressure.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.inclusion.percent
01-client-fs-io.description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. Any worker whose remaining pinned-cache capacity is above this percentage is always kept in the candidate pool, in addition to the top fraction. This lets a worker keep receiving writes until it is (100 minus this value) percent full, so a lightly loaded cluster spreads writes across every worker instead of parking the lowest-ranked ones.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.min.remaining.percent
01-client-fs-io.description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. Workers whose remaining pinned-cache capacity is below this percentage are dropped from the candidate pool (lowest-ratio first), but the pool is never reduced below the requested replica count.

01-client-fs-io.description: The space amplification percentage threshold that triggers write cache compaction. This value represents the allowed physical storage overhead relative to the logical file size as a percentage. For example, the default value of 50 means compaction is triggered when the physical file size reaches 150% of its logical size (i.e., the extra space occupied is 50% of the logical size). A value of 100 would mean compaction triggers when physical size is 200% of the logical size.

If a file's logical size is smaller than this threshold, compaction will not be triggered regardless of its space amplification ratio. This prevents unnecessary I/O overhead from compacting small files where the reclaimed space would be negligible.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.timeout
01-client-fs-io.description: Absolute deadline applied to the server-streaming listChildren RPC. Set generously because legitimate large-directory listings (e.g. prewarm of a million-file bucket) take real time; a stuck worker freezes the caller for up to this duration, but pre-fix behavior was an unbounded hang (AC-7179). Unary index service RPCs (addChild, removeChild, invalidate) use the generic alluxio.user.rpc.retry.max.duration timeout instead so they fail fast. Acts as a backstop alongside the per-batch inactivity timeout (alluxio.client.index.service.list.inactivity.timeout) — whichever fires first cancels the stream.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.inactivity.timeout
01-client-fs-io.description: Per-batch inactivity timeout for the server-streaming listChildren RPC. When > 0, cancels the stream if the server fails to deliver the next batch within this window. Because batches arriving from the server reset the timer, this lets legitimate long listings run indefinitely while still failing fast on a worker that is stuck or blackholed mid-stream — the freeze the user sees is bounded by this value, not by the absolute listChildren deadline. Default 0 disables the inactivity timer entirely; the absolute listChildren deadline (alluxio.client.index.service.list.timeout) is then the only bound. The default is conservative pending the worker-side cancellation handling tracked under AC-7212 — set to a positive value (e.g. 30sec) to opt in once the worker side is ready.

01-client-fs-io.group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
01-client-fs-io.name: alluxio.client.index.service.client.cache.idle.timeout
01-client-fs-io.description: Idle timeout for cached index service clients. Entries that have not been accessed for this duration are evicted and their underlying gRPC channel is closed. Bounds long-tail resource accumulation when worker pods churn (every dead pod that the cache talked to would otherwise keep its channel alive forever). Default 1day favors avoiding spurious reconnects on sparse FUSE workloads at the cost of some log noise from dead-channel reconnect attempts (~720/day per evicted pod). Lower (e.g. 1h) for high-churn environments where many pods are recycled per day.

02-client-cache.group_title: 客户端本地缓存 / 元数据缓存
02-client-cache.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.put.staging.enabled
02-client-cache.description: If true, LocalPageStore.put() writes the page to a staging file under the cache dir's .staging/ subdirectory and atomically renames it into place. Preserves snapshot consistency for in-flight POSIX-pinned zero-copy reads when the same page is concurrently rewritten (e.g. passive cache / re-load), and prevents leaving a 0-byte stub at the page path if the disk hits ENOSPC mid-write. If false, falls back to in-place O_TRUNC + write. Default true; disable only to mitigate a regression specific to staging-based writes.

02-client-cache.group_title: 客户端本地缓存 / 元数据缓存
02-client-cache.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled
02-client-cache.description: If true, the local page store writes staged pages with direct I/O (O_DIRECT), bypassing the OS page cache. Because the page store is itself a cache, buffered writes double-cache the data and pollute the page cache; direct I/O avoids that and reduces the CPU spent copying data into the kernel page cache, at the cost of an extra user-space copy into an aligned buffer. Only the staging write path is affected; metadata and append writes stay buffered. Direct I/O is only supported on Linux filesystems that allow O_DIRECT (e.g. ext4, xfs); on an unsupported filesystem the staged page write fails and the error is surfaced, so disable this option there. Applies to both the worker page store and the client-side cache.

02-client-cache.group_title: 客户端本地缓存 / 元数据缓存
02-client-cache.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.min.size
02-client-cache.description: Minimum staged page write size for using direct I/O. When alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled is true, writes smaller than this threshold use the buffered write path instead. Set to 0 to allow direct I/O for all staged page writes.

02-client-cache.name: <unresolved:USER_CLIENT_CACHE_LOCAL_STORE_DIRECT_IO_FOR_WRITE_CACHE_ONLY_ENABLED>
02-client-cache.description: If true, direct I/O is restricted to write-cache pages, which come from page-aligned pooled buffers and benefit most from O_DIRECT; all other pages (e.g. read-cache fills) use the buffered write path. If false, the file id is not checked and any page may take the direct I/O path. Only has an effect when alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled is true.

04-worker-page-store.group_title: Worker Page Store 存储引擎
04-worker-page-store.name: alluxio.worker.data.load.buffer.size
04-worker-page-store.description: The read buffer size for the buffered (non-streaming) distributed-load path (see ={@code false}): one UFS read covers this many bytes and is held in direct memory while its pages are inserted. If less than the page size it is raised to the page size. Unused by the streaming path, which reads one page at a time from a single per-partition stream.

04-worker-page-store.group_title: Worker Page Store 存储引擎
04-worker-page-store.name: alluxio.worker.data.load.streaming.enabled
04-worker-page-store.description: If true (default), distributed load reads each file through a single per-partition stream (one ranged GET per partition on object stores) and inserts it one page at a time, so the UFS read granularity is decoupled from resident memory: direct memory is one page per concurrent load task, independent of the GET size. 05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.backpressure.timeout
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Maximum time a heavy-pool thread will wait for the Netty channel to become writable (backpressure) before aborting with an IOException. Prevents heavy-pool threads from being pinned indefinitely by hung or slow TCP clients. Set to 0 to disable the timeout (not recommended).

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.core.thread.number
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Core thread number for async heavy thread pool. Defaults to the same value as the maximum thread number so that ThreadPoolExecutor never calls Thread.start() from the caller (event loop) thread on the hot path. Sized to absorb concurrent HEAVY-tag S3 ops (GetObject, PutObject, CopyObject, UploadPartCopy) under high connection counts (~512 MB stack).

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.queue.size

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.bucketpathcache.timeout
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Per-worker TTL for the positive cache that records whether an Alluxio S3 bucket path exists and is a directory. Disabled by default ("0min") to match AWS S3 read-after-write consistency for bucket lifecycle. The cache is consulted by the bucket-existence checks that remain on the bucket-level metadata path (HeadBucket / GetBucketLocation / GetBucketVersioning / ListMultipartUploads / DeleteBucket and the few remaining checkPathIsAlluxioDirectory call sites in object-level operations); enabling it (e.g. "60s") trades a bounded staleness window for fewer bucket-validate RPCs on those paths. Only positive (exists) entries are cached — missing buckets are not negatively cached so a freshly-created bucket isn't masked until TTL expiry. The 404-disambiguation helpers in NettyRestUtils do not consult this cache; they always re-probe.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.bucket.naming.restrictions.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Toggles whether or not the Alluxio S3 API will enforce AWS S3 bucket naming restrictions. See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucketnamingrules.html.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.list.directory.as.object.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Toggles whether the Alluxio S3 API will include directories as 0-byte objects in ListObjects/ListObjectsV2 responses. When true (the default), every directory encountered during listing is returned as a 0-byte entry in Contents (Alluxio's historical behavior). When false, the prefix path itself is suppressed and synthesized parent folders (folders with no backing UFS object, identified by a zero lastModificationTimeMs) are dropped, while real 0-byte directory marker objects (folders carrying a positive mtime plumbed through from the underlying object's LastModified) are preserved. Listings with a delimiter are unaffected: directories continue to surface via CommonPrefixes.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.complete.multipart.upload.min.part.size
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: The minimum required file size of parts for multipart uploads. Parts which are smaller than this limit aside from the final part will result in an EntityTooSmall error code. Set to 0 to disable size requirements.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.connection.keep.alive.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: If enabled the connection keep-alive, S3 API will maintain the TCP connection to support the client to reuse it and send new requests.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.connection.idle.max.time
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Specify the max keep-alive time for the HTTP connection in S3 API. If the channel has been idle for the specified time, the connection will be closed. If set it as 0, the connection will not actively expire.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.deletetype
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Delete type when deleting buckets and objects through S3 API. Valid options are `%s` (delete both in Alluxio and UFS), `%s` (delete only the buckets or objects in Alluxio namespace).

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.header.metadata.max.size
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: The maximum size to allow for user-defined metadata in S3 PUTrequest headers. Set to 0 to disable size limits.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.only.read.cache.data.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: If enabled, readObject request can only read data when the data are cached in Alluxio. If the data are not cached, it will return the not-found error.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.redirect.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: If enabled, readObject request may redirect the request to another appropriate server based on the consistent hash ring and cache load situation.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.write.redirect.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: If enabled, write requests may be redirected to another appropriate server based on the consistent hash ring and cache load situation.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.redirect.health.check.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: When enabled, an RPC is used to verify the health of the worker before redirecting to S3. Note: This will introduce significant overhead in both latency and throughput.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.tagging.restrictions.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Toggles whether or not the Alluxio S3 API will enforce AWS S3 tagging restrictions (10 tags, 128 character keys, 256 character values) See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/tagging-managing.html.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.receive.packet.size
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Packet size for each split chunk of the s3 request.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.transfer.packet.size
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Packet size for each transferred and flushed data.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.tls.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Set it to true to enable the TLS on S3 API.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.writetype
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Write type when creating buckets and objects through S3 API. Currently, only `CACHE_THROUGH` (try to cache, write to UnderFS synchronously), and `THROUGH` (no cache, write to UnderFS synchronously). are supported

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.put.object.calculate.md5.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Whether calculate md5 on S3 API.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.s3.tls.mutual.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Set it to true to enable the mutual TLS on S3 API.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.http.server.enabled
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Whether to enable worker HTTP server.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.http.server.port
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: The port Alluxio's worker's HTTP server runs on.

05-worker-s3-gateway.name: alluxio.worker.http.server.connection.idle.timeout
05-worker-s3-gateway.description: Idle timeout for connections on the worker HTTP server (the :28080 REST / OCI-mirror pipeline). When a connection has neither read nor written for this long, it is closed, reclaiming file descriptors from clients that vanished without sending a TCP RST (container OOM, NAT drop). Active transfers keep the channel non-idle so they are not affected. 0 disables idle detection.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.buffer.send

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.buffer.receive

06-worker-net-rpc.description: The device name Alluxio's worker rpc service binds to.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.web.bind.device
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The device name Alluxio's worker web binds to.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.bind.host
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The hostname Alluxio's worker node binds to.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.grpc.writer.threads.max
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of threads used to write files in the data server.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.ufs.reader.threads.max
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of threads used to load files from UFS.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.writer.buffer.size.messages
06-worker-net-rpc.description: When a client writes to a remote worker, the maximum number of data messages to buffer by the server for each request.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.threads.core
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The number of core threads used to read pages in the netty data server.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.threads.max
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of threads used to read pages in the netty data server.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.thread.keep.alive.time
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Idle keep-alive time for non-core threads in the netty reader pool.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.queue.size
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The size of the queue in the reader thread pool

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.queue.type
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Backing queue for the netty reader thread pool. HANDOFF_QUEUE pairs with BoundedHandoffExecutor; other values use a plain ThreadPoolExecutor.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.queue.size
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The size of the queue in the writer thread pool. Shared by both the writer and UFS-writer pools.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.queue.type
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Backing queue for the netty writer thread pool. HANDOFF_QUEUE pairs with BoundedHandoffExecutor; other values use a plain ThreadPoolExecutor.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.ufs.writer.queue.type
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Backing queue for the netty UFS-writer thread pool. HANDOFF_QUEUE pairs with BoundedHandoffExecutor; other values use a plain ThreadPoolExecutor. Queue size (when bounded) comes from .

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.queue.enabled
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Whether netty buffer queue is enabled

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.threads.core
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The number of core threads used to write pages in the netty data server. Shared by both the writer and UFS-writer pools.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.threads.max
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of threads used to write pages in the netty data server.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.thread.keep.alive.time
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Idle keep-alive time for non-core threads in the netty writer pool. Shared by both the writer and UFS-writer pools.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.ufs.writer.threads.max
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of threads used to write files to UFS in the netty data server.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.buffer.size.packets
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of parallel data packets when a client writes to a worker.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.handler.thread.idle.timeout
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum time a worker keeps a handler thread ready for listening to client requests on an open channel. If no requests occur within this time, the idle thread is released. Shorter values improve thread utilization but may incur a scheduling overhead when a new request comes in after the thread has been released.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.buffer.size.packets
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of parallel data packets when a client reads from a worker.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.watchdog.enabled
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Whether the worker periodically logs in-flight Netty read requests that have been stuck in a non-terminal state longer than . Logging only; helps diagnose reads that never terminate (and so never emit a close log).

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.watchdog.stuck.threshold
06-worker-net-rpc.description: How long an in-flight Netty read request may make no progress (no state transition) before the watchdog reports it as wedged. Defaults to roughly twice (default 10s) — a read stalled longer than the client's per-response timeout, twice over. The scan interval is derived from this value, so there is no separate interval to configure.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.flowcontrol.window
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The HTTP2 flow control window used by worker gRPC connections. Larger value will allow more data to be buffered but will use more memory.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.keepalive.time
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The amount of time for data server (for block reads and block writes) to wait for a response before pinging the client to see if it is still alive.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.keepalive.timeout
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum time for a data server (for block reads and block writes) to wait for a keepalive response before closing the connection.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.permit.keepalive.time
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Specify the most aggressive keep-alive time clients are permitted to configure. The server will try to detect clients exceeding this rate and when detected will forcefully close the connection.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.max.inbound.message.size
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The max inbound message size used by worker gRPC connections.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.boss.threads
06-worker-net-rpc.description: How many threads to use for accepting new requests.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.channel
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Netty channel type: NIO or EPOLL. If EPOLL is not available, this will automatically fall back to NIO.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.file.transfer
06-worker-net-rpc.description: When returning files to the user, select how the data is transferred; valid options are `MAPPED` (uses java MappedByteBuffer) and `TRANSFER` (uses Java FileChannel.transferTo).

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.shutdown.quiet.period
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The quiet period. When the netty server is shutting down, it will ensure that no RPCs occur during the quiet period. If an RPC occurs, then the quiet period will restart before shutting down the netty server.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.watermark.high
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Determines how many bytes can be in the write queue before switching to non-writable.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.watermark.low
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Once the high watermark limit is reached, the queue must be flushed down to the low watermark before switching back to writable.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.file.region.backpressure.enabled
06-worker-net-rpc.description: If true, count FileRegion bytes toward the netty data-server's outbound buffer watermark, so WriteBufferWaterMark backpressure bounds per-channel FileRegion queue depth. Bounds the worker's open-FD count per in-flight S3 GET regardless of object size. Disable only as an emergency rollback if the size-estimator change interacts badly with a specific workload; revert restores the previous behaviour where FileRegions are treated as zero-sized and the outbound queue can grow with response size.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.worker.threads
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Number of threads to use for processing requests in worker

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.reader.buffer.size
06-worker-net-rpc.description: When a client reads from a remote worker, the maximum amount of data not received by client allowed before the worker pauses sending more data. If this value is lower than read chunk size, read performance may be impacted as worker waits more often for buffer to free up. Higher value will increase the memory consumed by each read request.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.reader.buffer.pooled
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Whether it is using pooled direct buffer or unpooled wrapped buffer when creating a buffer for remote read

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.reader.max.chunk.size.bytes
06-worker-net-rpc.description: When a client read from a remote worker, the maximum chunk size.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.shutdown.timeout
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Maximum amount of time to wait until the worker gRPC server is shutdown (regardless of the quiet period).

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.network.zerocopy.enabled
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Whether zero copy is enabled on worker when processing data streams.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.port
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The port for Alluxio worker's RPC service.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.web.bind.host
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The hostname Alluxio worker's web server binds to.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.web.hostname
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The hostname Alluxio worker's web UI binds to.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.web.port
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The port Alluxio worker's web UI runs on.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.type
06-worker-net-rpc.description: Type of ExecutorService for Alluxio worker gRPC server. Supported values are TPE (for ThreadPoolExecutor) and FJP (for ForkJoinPool).

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.core.pool.size
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The number of threads to keep in thread pool of worker RPC ExecutorService.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.max.pool.size
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The maximum number of threads allowed for worker RPC ExecutorService. When the maximum is reached, attempts to replace blocked threads fail.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.keepalive
06-worker-net-rpc.description: The keep alive time of a thread in worker RPC ExecutorServicelast used before this thread is terminated (and replaced if necessary).

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.tpe.queue.type
06-worker-net-rpc.description: This property is effective when %s is set to TPE. It specifies the internal task queue that's used by RPC ExecutorService. Supported values are: LINKED_BLOCKING_QUEUE, ARRAY_BLOCKING_QUEUE and SYNCHRONOUS_BLOCKING_QUEUE

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.tpe.queue.size
06-worker-net-rpc.description: This property is effective when %s is set to TPE and %s is set to one of the bounded queue types: ,. It specifies the size of the internal task queue that's used by RPC ExecutorService.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.tpe.allow.core.threads.timeout
06-worker-net-rpc.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ThreadPoolExecutor. It controls whether core threads can timeout and terminate when there is no work. Must be false when %s is HANDOFF_QUEUE, which pre-starts a core worker to keep the pool warm.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.fjp.parallelism
06-worker-net-rpc.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. It controls the parallelism level (internal queue count) of master RPC ExecutorService.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.fjp.min.runnable
06-worker-net-rpc.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. It controls the minimum allowed number of core threads not blocked. A value of 1 ensures liveness. A larger value might improve throughput but might also increase overhead.

06-worker-net-rpc.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.fjp.async
06-worker-net-rpc.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. if true, it establishes local first-in-first-out scheduling mode for forked tasks that are never joined. This mode may be more appropriate than default locally stack-based mode in applications in which worker threads only process event-style asynchronous tasks.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.enabled
08-worker-data-accel.description: Whether to enable the OCI Distribution V2 registry mirror (/v2/...) on the worker HTTP server. When enabled, the worker fronts an upstream OCI registry such as Azure ACR. See design/Azure-ACR/alluxio_acr_v02_refined_full_design_cn.md.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.upstream.allowlist
08-worker-data-accel.description: Comma-separated allowlist of upstream registry hostnames the OCI mirror is permitted to forward to (e.g. "myacr.azurecr.io,secondacr.azurecr.io"). Empty means "allow any host" -- useful for development but unsafe in production, since a misconfigured oci-proxy could otherwise direct the worker to fetch from arbitrary internet hosts.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.persistent.cache.enabled
08-worker-data-accel.description: Whether the OCI registry handler decides cache hits by probing the worker page store directly (via CacheManager.getUsage) instead of consulting the in-memory mBlobMetaCache. When true, blob bytes that survive a worker restart can still be served from the on-disk cache, at the cost of one upstream HEAD request per cold (in-memory) lookup. Recommended for production once validated.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.blacklist.etcd.path
08-worker-data-accel.description: Etcd parent path where the OCI mirror watches for cache-bypass entries. Each child key represents one URL-escaped `<registry>/<repo>` (exact-match) that should bypass both the in-memory manifest/blob meta caches and the worker page store. Used to opt out frequently-mutated images (e.g. tags like `latest`, `developer`, or large Windows base images) from cache logic so that tag rewrites take effect immediately. Workers update their view via an etcd watch.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.authz.ttl
08-worker-data-accel.description: TTL of the repo-level AuthZ short cache (positive verdicts). Shorter values make permission revocations propagate faster at the cost of more upstream auth checks. Tokens that expire sooner than this value will still be honoured by the cache up to the configured TTL, so do not raise it above the typical upstream token lifetime.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.authz.negative.ttl
08-worker-data-accel.description: TTL of negative (deny) AuthZ cache entries. Implemented via a scheduled invalidate so a deny verdict yields the cache sooner than a normal positive entry; lets a user that just received new permissions pull successfully without waiting for the full positive TTL.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.binding.ttl
08-worker-data-accel.description: TTL of the repo-scoped object binding cache (digest ↔ repo safety fence) AND of the blob metadata cache. The two are intentionally linked: a metadata entry must never outlive its binding, otherwise the handler could serve cached bytes whose authorization fence has expired. Longer values reduce upstream HEAD traffic; shorter values force more frequent re-validation.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.tag.soft.ttl
08-worker-data-accel.description: Soft TTL for tag → digest bindings. Below this age, cached bindings are served without contacting upstream. After this age but before the hard TTL, the binding is still served but the next request triggers an upstream HEAD revalidation so a tag that has been re-pushed is detected promptly. Must be ≤ the hard TTL.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.tag.hard.ttl
08-worker-data-accel.description: Hard TTL for tag → digest bindings. After this age the entry is fully evicted and the next request takes the slow GET-manifest path.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.singleflight.timeout
08-worker-data-accel.description: Maximum time a blob singleflight follower waits for the leader's cold pull to populate the cache before falling through to its own direct upstream fetch. A follower fall-through costs one extra upstream GET; the old 10min value could stall a stampede of clients on a wedged leader. Raise it only for genuinely huge (multi-GB) layers over a slow upstream.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.stream.timeout
08-worker-data-accel.description: Upper bound a blob streamer thread waits for a single chunk write to the client channel to complete under backpressure. Caps how long a slow or vanished client can pin a streamer thread; pairs with the HTTP idle timeout.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.range.prefetch.threads
08-worker-data-accel.description: Max concurrent background full-blob backfills triggered by range misses. Saturation skips the backfill (the range was already served from upstream); raise for K8s cold-start bursts where many nodes range-miss the same large image at once.

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.range.prefetch.queue
08-worker-data-accel.description: Queue capacity for the range-miss blob backfill pool (see ).

08-worker-data-accel.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.upstream.throttle.cooldown
08-worker-data-accel.description: When the upstream registry returns a 429/503 rate-limit response, the worker records a short per-(registry, repo) cooldown and serves an immediate 429 (with Retry-After) to further requests for that repo instead of hammering the already-throttling upstream. The cooldown honors the upstream Retry-After header but is capped at this value. 0 disables the local cooldown (Retry-After is still propagated to clients).

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.oci.registry.credential.provider
12-ufs-backends.description: Name of the OciCredentialProvider used to mint an upstream OCI registry pull credential when there is no client-supplied token (image preload). Selected by name from the SPI implementations registered under alluxio.oci.auth.OciCredentialProviderFactory. The default 'azure-workload-identity' performs the keyless Azure Workload Identity -> ACR token exchange. Used by both the Coordinator (layout scan) and the worker (blob fetch); the serving path is unaffected and keeps using the client's token.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.oci.registry.static.token
12-ufs-backends.description: Pre-supplied upstream registry Bearer token used by the 'static-token' OCI credential provider. Defaults to the OCI_REGISTRY_STATIC_TOKEN environment variable. Intended for externally injected/short-lived tokens (e.g. an ACR access token) where the worker does not mint its own; the token is returned as-is for every repository.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.client.id
12-ufs-backends.description: Override for the Azure AD application (client) id used by the azure-workload-identity credential provider. Defaults to the AZURE_CLIENT_ID environment variable injected by the Workload Identity webhook; set this only to override that injected value.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.tenant.id
12-ufs-backends.description: Override for the Azure AD tenant id used by the azure-workload-identity credential provider. Defaults to the AZURE_TENANT_ID environment variable injected by the Workload Identity webhook.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.federated.token.file
12-ufs-backends.description: Override for the path to the kubelet-projected ServiceAccount token used as the client_assertion by the azure-workload-identity credential provider. Defaults to the AZURE_FEDERATED_TOKEN_FILE environment variable injected by the Workload Identity webhook.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.authority.host
12-ufs-backends.description: Azure AD authority host the azure-workload-identity credential provider posts the client_credentials grant to. Defaults to the public cloud; override (or set AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST) for sovereign clouds.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.acr.scope
12-ufs-backends.description: AAD scope identifying the ACR data plane, requested when the azure-workload-identity credential provider exchanges the projected token for an AAD access token. Must point at ACR; the ARM scope is rejected by ACR's /oauth2/exchange endpoint.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.multipart.upload.memory.buffer.pool.capacity
12-ufs-backends.description: The number of concurrent upload tasks the multipart uploader can conduct. Each upload task needs a buffer resource from the pool, to store temporary data before the data is written into HDFS. Increasing the parallelism improves the performance but also consumes more memory.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.multipart.upload.memory.buffer.byte.size
12-ufs-backends.description: The byte buffer size used by multipart upload. Once the buffer is full, the uploader will write the content of the buffer as a new file. Recommend setting this value to be the HDFS block size to achieve the best efficiency.

12-ufs-backends.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.multipart.upload.retry.attempts
12-ufs-backends.description: The number of retry attempts can be done for upload a single part of the file.

13-coordinator-master.description: The capacity of the dead letter queue we persist failed tasks in memory for retrying purpose. Once the queue is full, failed subtasks will not retryand will just fail. On average one subtask takes up 0.5KB in memory. Properly set this property to avoid OOM.

13-coordinator-master.description: If the metastore is ROCKS, this property controls where the RocksDB stores job metadata. This property defaults to . And it can be used to change job metadata storage path to a different disk to improve RocksDB performance.

13-coordinator-master.group_title: Coordinator / Master 元数据与调度
13-coordinator-master.name: alluxio.master.metastore.rocks.exclusive.lock.timeout
13-coordinator-master.description: Before RocksDB is shut down/restarted/restored, Master will wait for ongoing operations to complete/abort. This timeout specifies how long to wait before forcing the action. Then the leftover operations will fail. Normally the wait will be short, because when master fails over/shuts down/replays journal, all other concurrent operations should have been stopped. This is just one extra safety guard. Therefore we do not recommend setting this manually.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.enabled
14-membership-etcd.description: Whether the cluster-wide per-user FUSE rate limiter is enabled. When false the limiter is not constructed and adds zero overhead on the I/O path.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.server.port
14-membership-etcd.description: The port the cluster rate-limit token server listens on (coordinator side) and that FUSE token clients connect to.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.fallback.to.local
14-membership-etcd.description: When the rate-limit token server is unreachable, whether each FUSE client falls back to enforcing the limit locally (fail-open). When false, requests block until the server is reachable again.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.fraction
14-membership-etcd.description: Fraction of a tenant's per-second limit that each FUSE client fetches from the token server per refill (one RPC amortized across the batch). Larger values mean fewer refill RPCs and lower stall risk (favoring throughput) but let a client hold more unused tokens locally (looser cross-client accuracy); smaller values are tighter but chattier. The result is clamped to the configured min/max batch size and never exceeds the per-second limit itself.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.min.size
14-membership-etcd.description: Lower bound on the per-refill token batch a FUSE client fetches from the rate-limit token server.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.max.size
14-membership-etcd.description: Upper bound on the per-refill token batch a FUSE client fetches from the rate-limit token server. Caps how many tokens a single client can hold locally, bounding cross-client inaccuracy at the cost of more refill RPCs at very high limits.

Note that FDB still enforces a 5-second read version staleness window — transactions that take longer than 5s from their read version will fail with transaction_too_old regardless of this setting.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.time
14-membership-etcd.description: gRPC HTTP/2 keepalive ping interval for the jetcd client. When the channel is idle for this long, a PING is sent so a stalled etcd endpoint (e.g. during defrag) is detected and the client can recover the gRPC channel. Must be >= the etcd server's --grpc-keepalive-min-time (default 5s on etcd) or the server will send GOAWAY frames. To preserve an in-flight lease across a stall, this value plus alluxio.etcd.keepalive.timeout should be less than the lease TTL (alluxio.worker.failure.detection.timeout for workers, default 15s); otherwise this setting only bounds recovery time, it does not save the lease. With defaults (10s+3s=13s detection, 15s lease) the margin is a tight 2s — lower this to 5s for a 7s margin if your network can tolerate the extra keepalive PINGs.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.timeout
14-membership-etcd.description: How long the jetcd client waits for a keepalive PONG before marking the gRPC channel as broken. Lower values detect stalled endpoints faster but increase false-positive disconnects on lossy networks. Total detection latency is alluxio.etcd.keepalive.time + this value.

14-membership-etcd.group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
14-membership-etcd.name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.without.calls
14-membership-etcd.description: Whether the jetcd client sends keepalive pings even when no RPCs are in flight. Required for failover when the only active stream (e.g. LeaseKeepAlive) is itself stalled on a hung etcd endpoint.

14-membership-etcd.description: The capacity in bytes of the RocksDB block metadata table LRU cache. If unset, the RocksDB default will be used. 16-fuse.description: The class name of the authorizer used for FUSE authorization

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.authorization.user.group.translation.enabled
16-fuse.description: Whether to enable user/group translation in FUSE authorization.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.auth.policy.class
16-fuse.description: The fuse auth policy class. Valid options include: `alluxio.fuse.auth.LaunchUserGroupAuthPolicy` using the user launching the AlluxioFuse application to do authentication, `alluxio.fuse.auth.SystemUserGroupAuthPolicy` using the end-user running the fuse command to do authentication which matches POSIX standard but sacrifices performance, `alluxio.fuse.auth.CustomAuthPolicy` using the custom user group to do authentication.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.auth.policy.custom.user
16-fuse.description: The fuse user name for custom auth policy. Only valid if the is alluxio.fuse.auth.CustomAuthPolicy

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.auth.policy.custom.group
16-fuse.description: The fuse group name for custom auth policy. Only valid if the is alluxio.fuse.auth.CustomAuthPolicy

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.cached.paths.max
16-fuse.description: Maximum number of FUSE-to-Alluxio path mappings to cache for FUSE conversion.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.debug.enabled
16-fuse.description: Run FUSE in debug mode, and have the fuse process log every FS request.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.dir.stream.cache.size
16-fuse.description: Number of child entries cached during a directory listing

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.fs.name
16-fuse.description: The FUSE file system name.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.use.create.file.options.overwrite.flag
16-fuse.description: When this configuration is set to true, Fuse will use the overwrite flag in createFileOptions when overwriting files instead of directly deleting the file, which helps reduce the number of metadata requests.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.request.hard.timeout
16-fuse.description: If enabled, FUSE requests will be failed immediately when the timeout is reached. Only takes effect if the timeout is > 0

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.return.timeout.error.on.request.hard.timeout
16-fuse.description: Whether return timeout error code on timeout. This provides the user a more explicit error but may cause unexpected application retry behavior.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.logging.threshold
16-fuse.description: Logging a FUSE API call when it takes more time than the threshold.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.max.reader.concurrency
16-fuse.description: Max number of concurrent readers per file in FUSE

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.read.write.lock.manager.try.lock.timeout
16-fuse.description: Timeout for read write lockmanager trylock operation.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.mount.alluxio.path
16-fuse.description: The Alluxio path to mount to the given Fuse mount point configured by %s.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.mount.options
16-fuse.description: The platform specific Fuse mount options to mount the given Fuse mount point. If multiple mount options are provided, separate them with comma.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.mount.point
16-fuse.description: The absolute local filesystem path that mount Alluxio path to.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.umount.timeout
16-fuse.description: The timeout to wait for all in progress file read and write to finish before unmounting the Fuse filesystem when SIGTERM signal is received. A value smaller than or equal to zero means no umount wait time.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.list.timeout
16-fuse.description: The timeout to wait for list dir. A value smaller than or equal to zero means wait for readdir until finished.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.low.level.active.inode.attr.cache.ttl
16-fuse.description: The maximum age of a retained low-level active inode snapshot that can be returned directly from the low-level FUSE active inode table without refreshing metadata from the backend. A value smaller than or equal to zero disables this userspace inode attribute cache.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.user.group.translation.enabled
16-fuse.description: Whether to translate Alluxio users and groups into Unix users and groups when exposing Alluxio files through the FUSE API. When this property is set to false, the user and group for all FUSE files will match the user who started the alluxio-fuse process.Note that this applies to JNR-FUSE only.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.authorization.user.cache.expiration.time
16-fuse.description: The expiration time for the cached user authorization info in FUSE.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.authorization.user.cache.max.size
16-fuse.description: The maximum number of users to cache authorization info for in FUSE.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.special.command.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, user can issue special FUSE commands by using 'ls -l /path/to/fuse_mount/.alluxiocli.<command_name>.<subcommand_name>', For example, when the Alluxio is mounted at local path /mnt/alluxio-fuse, 'ls -l /mnt/alluxio-fuse/.alluxiocli.metadatacache.dropAll' will drop all the user metadata cache. 'ls -l /mnt/alluxio-fuse/.alluxiocli.metadatacache.size' will get the metadata cache size， the size value will be show in the output's filesize field. 'ls -l /mnt/alluxio-fuse/path/to/be/cleaned/.alluxiocli.metadatacache.drop' will drop the metadata cache of path '/mnt/alluxio-fuse/path/to/be/cleaned/'

16-fuse.name: <unresolved:FUSE_POSIX_LOCK_ENABLED>
16-fuse.description: Whether to enable distributed POSIX locks in FUSE V2. Requires alluxio.write.cache.dual.buffer.file.system.type to be GENERIC_FDB_BACKED_V2.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.fast.copy.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, ignore chmod and chown for fast copying.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.enable.read.dir.plus
16-fuse.description: If enabled, the fuse will read dir by offset.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.read.dir.plus.batch.size
16-fuse.description: the batch size of read dir by offset.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.enforce.direct.io.read
16-fuse.description: If enabled, fuse will enforce the direct io mode for read only files.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.local.backed.stream.tmp.dir
16-fuse.description: The local temporary directory for FUSE local backed stream.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.local.backed.stream.tmp.dir.capacity
16-fuse.description: The maximum disk usage for FUSE local backed stream.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.local.backed.stream.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, FUSE will use multiplex read/write random access stream to serve FUSE read/write requests. This feature aims to replace the legacy and to provide better posix compatibility.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.local.backed.stream.buffer.size
16-fuse.description: This buffer size is used in FuseLocalBackedStream when copying the UFS file to local and copying the local file to UFS.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.open.file.reference.creation.wait.previous.close.timeout
16-fuse.description: Timeout for creating open file reference in the open file registry, when there is a previous close operation on the same file path in progress.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.silly.rename.interceptor.enabled
16-fuse.description: Manually manages the 'silly rename' behavior in FUSE. By enabling this, Alluxio intercepts rename requests that would create '.fuse_hidden' files. It enforces immediate deletion of the original file during a rename and ignores (no-op) any explicit delete calls on existing '.fuse_hidden' files to prevent unnecessary metadata operations.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.open.read.status.cache.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, caches file metadata (URIStatus) for 1 second to avoid repeated getStatus RPCs when the same read-only file is opened frequently. Only used for read-only file opens, not for getattr.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.in.memory.workspace.metastore.state.ufs.path
16-fuse.description: The UFS path used to restore and persist the in-memory workspace metastore state for FUSE when the metastore implementation is InMemoryFdbMetastoreImpl.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.in.memory.workspace.metastore.state.persist.interval
16-fuse.description: The fixed-rate interval for periodically persisting the in-memory workspace metastore state to .

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.in.memory.workspace.metastore.logical.size.quota
16-fuse.description: The logical file length quota enforced per in-memory workspace metastore instance.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.in.memory.workspace.metastore.inode.quota
16-fuse.description: The inode count quota enforced per in-memory workspace metastore instance.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.web.bind.host
16-fuse.description: The hostname Alluxio FUSE web UI binds to.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.web.hostname
16-fuse.description: The hostname of Alluxio FUSE web UI.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.web.port
16-fuse.description: The port Alluxio FUSE web UI runs on.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.non.disruptive.migration.enabled
16-fuse.description: Whether enable fuse non-disruptive migration.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.non.disruptive.migration.state.file.directory.path
16-fuse.description: The local directory path for save/restore fuse migration state files. If we are in k8s env, make sure both of new fuse pod and old fuse can access this directory.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.fuse.migration.ongoing.request.grace_period
16-fuse.description: The grace period for ongoing requests before migration. Timed out requests will be aborted forcefully.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.random.access.file.stream.buffer.size
16-fuse.description: This buffer size is used in RandomAccessFuseFileStream when copying the UFS file to local and copying the local file to UFS.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.random.access.file.stream.truncate.immediately.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, RandomAccessFuseFileStream will truncate the file to UFS immediately when processing truncate(0) operation.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.force.random.access.write.stream.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, RandomAccessFuseFileStream which support random write operation would be always used when writing file in FUSE.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.random.access.file.stream.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, RandomAccessFuseFileStream which support random write operation would be used when writing an existing file in FUSE.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.random.access.file.stream.atomic.write.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, RandomAccessFuseFileStream will use atomic write feature to upload file.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.cache.defer.open.file.attr.update.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, chmod/chown/utimens on an already-open write-cache file will stay local until they can piggyback on an existing write-cache metadata commit, with close as the fallback commit point.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.cache.async.delete.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, deleted/overwritten inodes are queued for asynchronous page-store cleanup instead of blocking the delete/rename path.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.cache.async.delete.max.pending.file.count
16-fuse.description: Maximum number of inodes that can be queued for asynchronous page-store cleanup. When this limit is reached, new deletes fall back to synchronous cleanup.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.cache.async.delete.max.pending.file.size
16-fuse.description: Maximum total file size (bytes) of inodes that can be queued for asynchronous page-store cleanup. When this limit is reached, new deletes fall back to synchronous cleanup.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.cache.async.delete.thread.count
16-fuse.description: Maximum number of threads in the async delete executor pool used to clean up page-store files in the background.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.intercept.system.xattr.get.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, FUSE getxattr requests for well-known probe xattrs such as security.capability and system.posix_acl_* are answered locally with ENODATA instead of querying the backing filesystem.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.dir
16-fuse.description: If specified, this directory is used for holding writeback enabled writes in FUSE.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.dir.quota
16-fuse.description: Quota for writeback cache directory. Setting values less than or equal to 0 represents no limitation.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.dir.quota.max.usage.ratio
16-fuse.description: The maximum ratio of quota utilized by writeback, exceeding this ratio, new write requests will degrade to regular writes instead of using writeback.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.allow.overwrite.uploading.files
16-fuse.description: This setting is used to configure whether overwriting files currently being uploaded is allowed. If this value is set to false, writeback will throw an error when writing to the file if it detects that another file with the same path is currently being uploaded.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.sync.flush.empty.file
16-fuse.description: If this configuration is set to true, when writeback detects that the file is empty, it will synchronize creation in UFS.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.degraded.sync.write.on.insufficient.space
16-fuse.description: When this configuration is enabled, writeback will degrade to synchronous writing when space is insufficient, instead of throwing an exception directly.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.max.upload.tasks
16-fuse.description: Maximum allowable asynchronous upload tasks for writeback. Exceeding this value will degrade to synchronous upload. Please note that this value is only a soft limit.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.max.acceleration.threads
16-fuse.description: The maximum number of threads for local writeback, with all writeback requests sharing these threads.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.buffer.size
16-fuse.description: Memory buffer size for writeback.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.status.bloom.filter.enabled
16-fuse.description: Whether using a Bloom filter to filter status, which could improve the performance of writing small files.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.write.back.status.bloom.filter.refresh.period
16-fuse.description: Bloom filter refresh period.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.multipart.upload.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, when writing a file to UFS by fuse, data will be partitioned into multiple parts and uploaded in parallel. This improves the write performance for large files if the network bandwidth is sufficient. This property tries to do multipart upload on a best effort. If the UFS doesn't support so, the upload will fall back to regular mode.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.sync.close.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, when closing a file in fuse, the file will be flushed into UFS synchronously, at the cost of degraded performance of following writes after the file is closed. It is rare but in some cases file can still be updated after it's closed. Known examples are fork(2), dup(2) and mmap(2)

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.fsync.enabled
16-fuse.description: [Experimental] If enabled, the fuse fsync operation will be handled, calling the internal alluxio flush implementation.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.symlink.enabled
16-fuse.description: Whether enable symlink.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.hard.link.return.exdev.enabled
16-fuse.description: Enables a workaround for RocksDB versions older than 7.1.1 that do not handle EOPNOTSUPP correctly. When enabled, Alluxio FUSE will return EXDEV instead of EOPNOTSUPP for hard link operations. This is primarily needed for compatibility with older RocksDB versions (e.g., dependencies in integration tests). Newer RocksDB versions do not require this setting.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.cross.quota.domain.return.exdev.enabled
16-fuse.description: Controls the errno FUSE returns when a rename or hard link is rejected because the source and destination are in different write-cache directory-quota domains. When false (default), it returns EOPNOTSUPP and the operation fails outright without copying. When true, it returns EXDEV, so coreutils mv transparently falls back to copy+delete (the copy is re-created and counted in the destination domain).

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.hard.link.fallback.as.copy
16-fuse.description: If enabled, when creating a hard link in FUSE, the operation will fall back to copying the file instead of returning an error.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.read.on.incomplete.file.enabled
16-fuse.description: If enabled, fuse allows a file to be read while it is being created by another thread. If either the random write feature or position write feature is enabled, read-during-write is supported natively, regardless of the value set for this property.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.read.write.random.access.stream.enabled
16-fuse.description: Whether to use ReadWriteRandomAccessStream. This stream supports simultaneous reading and writing, and provides better performance for sequential read/write operations. Note that this configuration only takes effect when is set to true.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.read.write.random.access.stream.compatible.flush.enabled
16-fuse.description: When this configuration is set to true, during a flush or fsync operation, if the underlying stream supports flushing, the stream’s flush method will be invoked. When this configuration is set to false, the flush or fsync operation will close the underlying stream and reopen it, switching to random write mode.

16-fuse.name: alluxio.user.fuse.path.based.config.file.path
16-fuse.description: If specified, alluxio will load path based fuse config from the file.

17-security.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.default.duration

17-security.default: "43200sec") // 12 hours .setDescription("Maximum lifetime of a temporary session token."
