19-write-ttl-quota.description: The master polls all workers for the latest quota usage regularly. This property specifies the interval between two polls.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.quota.worker.heartbeat.timeout.ms
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The master polls all workers for the latest quota usage regularly. This property specifies the worker needs to respond the quota usage within this timeout.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.quota.evictor.capacity.modifier.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: This configuration tweaks the behavior of the evictor which restores quota usage. A positive value like 64MB means the evictor will try to free 64MiB ahead of time on each worker. A negative value like -1MB means the evictor will tolerate each worker to use 1MB more than the capacity. The default value is 1MB, which means after eviction, the quota usage will still be slightly more than the quota definition, so the quota stays full.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.ttl.policy.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether TTL policy feature is enabled

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.ttl.policy.etcd.polling.interval.ms
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Interval for polling ETCD for the latest TTL policy definitions. By default the interval is 3s.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to enable the write buffer feature. When enabled, writes will go through a temporary buffer on workers before being persisted to the underlying storage.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.dual.buffer.file.system.type
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The type of the dual write buffer file system.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.local.file.system.path
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The local file system path for write buffer.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: <unresolved:WRITE_BUFFER_ROCKSDB_QUORUM_WRITE_REGEX>
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The regex to match files that will be written with rocksdb quorum write

19-write-ttl-quota.name: <unresolved:WRITE_BUFFER_ROCKSDB_LOCAL_WRITE_REGEX>
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The regex to match files that will be written with to local only

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.file.metadata.storage
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The storage type for write buffer file metadata. Options are `MEMORY` for in-memory storage (for testing) and `FDB` for FoundationDB.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.check.orphan.timeout
19-write-ttl-quota.description: This configuration specifies the timeout duration for orphan file detection.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.buffer.async.persist.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to enable asynchronous persistence of files from the write buffer to the underlying storage. If false, files are persisted upon close.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.thread.pool.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The size of the thread pool for asynchronously persisting files from the write buffer to the underlying storage.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.submit.batch.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The batch size of submitting async persist tasks.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.schedule.period
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The scheduling period for the asynchronous persistence service to check for and persist files from the write buffer.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.cache.free.delay
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The delay time for releasing the cache after data persistence is completed. A value less than or equal to 0 disables delayed release.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.file.check.period
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The period for checking files in the write buffer. Files that have been in the buffer longer than this period will be prioritized for persistence.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to enable multipart upload for the write buffer file system. When enabled, large writes are buffered in memory and flushed as part files, then merged at the metadata level on close.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.part.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The size of each part in bytes for write buffer multipart upload.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.buffer.number
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The number of buffers in the pool for write buffer multipart upload. Set to 0 to disable pooling: a new buffer is allocated on demand and dropped on release instead of being reused.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.threads
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The number of threads used for uploading parts in write buffer multipart upload.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.max.buffers.per.stream
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The maximum number of buffers a single multipart upload stream can hold concurrently. If the stream has reached this limit, further buffer allocation will block until an in-flight part write completes and returns its buffer. Set to 0 for unlimited (no per-stream limit).

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scanner.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to enable the background write-cache TTL scanner that automatically purges expired files from the TRANSIENT write cache.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.refresh.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to allow the on-demand `ttl refresh` command, which walks a path prefix and retroactively stamps the write-cache TTL onto already-cached files. Disabled by default; enable deliberately, as a refresh can walk a large subtree.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.interval
19-write-ttl-quota.description: How often the write-cache TTL scanner runs. Defaults to 1h to match the read-cache TTL eviction cadence (alluxio.ttl.eviction.check.interval); lower it for more prompt write-cache reclaim at higher coordinator/FDB scan cost, but not below unless that floor is also lowered.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.interval.min
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Floor on the effective write-cache TTL scan interval; defaults to 1h to match the scan-interval default, so by default the scanner runs no more often than hourly. A configured below this floor is clamped up to it (with a warning), which also prevents an accidental tiny interval from tight-spinning the scanner. To scan more frequently, lower this floor too — do so cautiously: scanning faster than the drain can reclaim only adds coordinator/FDB load with no benefit.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.max.per.round
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Maximum number of expired files the write-cache TTL scanner processes per scheduled run.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.batch.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Number of TTL index entries fetched per FDB range-scan page inside the write-cache TTL scanner.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.threads
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Number of threads the write-cache TTL scanner uses to purge expired files concurrently within a round. Higher values drain a backlog faster; each thread runs independent per-inode FDB transactions, made safe by the unlink CAS. Set to 1 for the legacy serial scan.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to wrap the singleton FDB metastore instance with the local read-through cache.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.positive.ttl
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The TTL for positive entries in the write-cache metastore read-through cache.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.negative.ttl
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The TTL for negative entries in the write-cache metastore read-through cache. Set to 0sec to disable negative-entry caching.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.compaction.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to enable write log compaction for the write cache. When enabled, write logs within a block will be periodically merged to reduce the number of log entries and reclaim storage space.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.id.allocator.batch.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The number of IDs to allocate per FDB transaction in the inode and write log ID allocators. A larger batch reduces FDB transactions but increases the gap in ID sequences when a node restarts.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.worker.id.cache.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The maximum number of entries cached in each direction of the worker-id to long-reference mapping (see WorkerIdMapper). Bounds memory while avoiding FDB point lookups for recently seen worker references.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.block.size
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The block size used by the write cache. Write logs and page store files are organized and compacted at block-level granularity.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.cluster.name
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The name of the PFS cluster. Used to namespace FDB subspaces so that multiple clusters can share the same FoundationDB instance.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.scatter.buckets
19-write-ttl-quota.description: The number of scatter buckets used by the inode ID allocator. This value is persisted in the cluster info table on first initialization and subsequent restarts will use the persisted value.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.cache.refresh.interval
19-write-ttl-quota.description: How often the in-memory directory-quota cache is reloaded from FoundationDB. The cache is consulted on every write hot path instead of issuing per-write quota reads, so a shorter interval yields tighter enforcement at the cost of more background scans. setDirectoryQuota and clearDirectoryQuota refresh the cache synchronously in the calling process; other processes (e.g. the coordinator when a mutation is issued from a CLI JVM) catch up on their next scheduled tick — i.e. within this interval.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.counter.anchor.bits
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Log2 of the number of anchor positions each directory-quota usage counter is scattered across in the FoundationDB inode keyspace (2^value anchors; default 8 = 256). More anchors spread a hot quota's counter writes over more storage teams, but cost proportionally more reads on cache refresh and getDirectoryQuota, so match it to the cluster's storage-team count. Expressed as a power of two so it can only change by integer multiples across versions, keeping the anchor set nested (a rolling restart stays correct once complete); values below 8 are raised to 8.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.scan.batch
19-write-ttl-quota.description: How many child inodes a directory-quota backfill worker stamps (or strips) per FoundationDB transaction. The ceiling is driven by transaction-conflict waste, not FDB's 5s/10MB limits: a larger batch redoes more work when it collides with online writes, nested backfills, or async deletion on the same inodes. Lower it for deeply nested quotas or when backfill overlaps large recursive deletes.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.worker.threads
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Size of the directory-quota backfill worker thread pool. Bounds the number of concurrent backfill write transactions against FoundationDB.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.scan.interval
19-write-ttl-quota.description: How often the directory-quota backfill leader polls the backfill/unbackfill queues for items to dispatch and for COMPUTING/CLEARING quotas whose queues have drained.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.batch.delay
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Test/debug throttle: an artificial delay a directory-quota backfill worker sleeps after each processed batch, slowing the backfill (regardless of subtree shape) so the COMPUTING/CLEARING window stays open long enough to observe or exercise concurrent operations. Default 0 disables it; leave it 0 in production.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.orphan.cleanup.interval
19-write-ttl-quota.description: How often the directory-quota backfill leader sweeps leaked usage counters: counter slots whose quota id no longer has a limit row (left behind when a non-empty quota-bearing directory is deleted and its descendants' delete-time decrements recreate slots after the inline cleanup removed the row). The leak is benign for enforcement (a quota with no limit row is never loaded into the enforce cache), so this is a low-frequency, best-effort sweep that runs only when the leader's queues are idle.

19-write-ttl-quota.name: alluxio.concurrent.write.enabled
19-write-ttl-quota.description: Whether to enable concurrent writing. When this configuration is set to true, it will simultaneously change the behavior of both FUSE and the worker: FUSE will convert all write locks into read locks, and the worker’s handle will allow writing to multiple files concurrently.
